english: the skin of emaciated persons Med.; *ṅo gyaṅ-gyaṅ* (ngo gyang gyang) W. greenish-yellow (spelling dubious). -- sṅo-sgá (sngo sga) officinal herb, Wdṅ. (green ginger?) -- sṅo-ṅád (sngo ngad) v. ṅad (ngad). -- sṅo-lyáṅ (sngo lyang) bluish green. -- sṅo-tóg (sngo tog) Schr. 'unripe, sour, of fruits'(?); more corr.: green, unripe fruits. -- sṅo-drégs (sngo dregs) green mud or mire Sch. -- sṅo-nág (sngo nag) deep blue. -- sṅo-ba (sngo ba) 1. vb. to get green, verdant; 2. adj., also sṅó-bo (sngo bo), more frq. sṅón-po (sngon po), sṅón-mo (sngon mo) blue, green, also used of the livid colour of diseased or famished people Glr. -- sṅo-smán (sngo sman) a medicinal herb. -- sṅo-tsód (sngo tsod) vegetables; herbs. -- sṅo-ló (sngo lo) the leaf of a plant; Cs.: 'sṅo-ló čár-ba (sngo lo car ba) to become notorious'. -- sṅo-sáṅs (sngo sangs) pale blue e.g. of the sky; sṅo-sáṅs-ma (sngo sangs ma) night Sch.

Cs. also sṅód-pa (sngod pa), pf. bsṅos (bsngos), fut. bsṅo (bsngo), imp. sṅos (sngos), 1. to become green Cs. — 2. (Lex. परिणाम?) to bless, *ṅo-wa gyáb-če* (ngo wa gyab ce) W., though in most cases as a requital for a present given; Dzl. ཐ, 16: to bless, to pronounce a benediction, hence also in litanies the words of the priest seem to be indiscriminately called sṅó-ba (sngo ba), whereas the responses of the congregation of monks are termed mt'ún-_gyúr (mthun 'gyur); generally: to dedicate, devote, e.g. one's property to the dkón-mč'óg gsum (dkon mchog gsum), i.e. in reality to the priesthood; dgé-ba _gró-drug dón-du sṅos (dge ba 'gro drug don du sngos), to devote alms, charitable gifts, to the (temporal and eternal) welfare of beings. Mil.; also to design, to intend, ṅá-la bsṅós-pai gyu (nga la bsngos pai g.yu) the turkois intended for me (by you) Mil.; Dzl. ས, 3: sá-la k'áṅ-bu daṅ rín-po-č'ér bsṅós-nas rtsé-ba (sa la khang bu dang rin po cher bsngos nas rtse ba), fancying the earth to consist of cottages and jewels, and thus playing with it.
Lex., prob. pf. bsṅogs (bsngogs), fut. bsṅog (bsngog), imp. sṅogs (sngogs), to vex, to annoy; cf. skyo-ṅógs (skyo ngogs), skyo-sṅógs (skyo sngogs).

= sṅa (snga) and sṅan (sngan), formerly, before, previously; sṅon t'ós-na (sngon thos na) having formerly heard Dzl.; sṅon máṅ-du k'yér yaṅ (sngon mang du khyer yang) although you have taken a good deal with you before; sṅon _dás-pai (sngon 'das pai) or byúṅ-bai dús-na (byung bai dus na) in by-gone times, frq.; sṅon bcóm-ldan-_dás (sngon bcom ldan 'das) a former Buddha Glr.; sṅon mi dbúl-po de (sngon mi dbul po de) this man formerly poor Dzl.; bdág-las sṅon bdág-gi p'a (bdag las sngon bdag gi pha) my father before me (has...); sṅón-gyi (sngon gyi) adj. former, last; sṅón-ma (sngon ma) the former (when two persons or things are spoken of), sṅón-ma-rnams (sngon ma rnams) the former (persons or things) Glr.; beginning, lhá-k'aṅ _ǰíg-pai sṅón-ma lhá-sa-la byás-te (lha khang 'jig pai sngon ma lha sa la byas te) making a beginning with the destruction of the temples in Lhasa Glr.; sṅón-du (sngon du) adv. and postp., before, at the head, in advance, in the front of, sṅón-du _gró-ba (sngon du 'gro ba) to go before or in advance, to precede, also of words and letters; sṅón-du _ǰúg-pa (sngon du 'jug pa) to put or place before, Gram.; sṅón-la (sngon la) = sṅón-du (sngon du): sṅón-la soṅ (sngon la song) walk first! Mil.; stón-pai sṅón-du (ston pai sngon du) (he died) before the Teacher (Buddha) Tar.; sooner, earlier, before the time supposed, sṅón-la ts'ár-ro (sngon la tshar ro) they were first in finishing (their task) Glr.; _o-ná sṅón-la _di p'úl čig ('o na sngon la 'di phul cig) oh yes, but first give me that Mil.; sṅón-nas (sngon nas) from a former time, from the beginning Mil.; sṅón-bźin (sngon bzhin) as formerly Mil. Comp. sṅon-skyés (sngon skyes) the first-born, eldest son. -- sṅon-gró (sngon 'gro) v. _gró-ba ('gro ba) compounds. -- sṅon-čád (sngon cad), sṅon-č'ád (sngon chad) Dzl., v. sṅan-č'ád (sngan chad). -- sṅon-_ǰúg (sngon 'jug) a prefixed letter Gram. -- sṅon-dús (sngon dus), sṅon-ts'é (sngon tshe) antiquity; adv. anciently, in times of old. -- sṅon-byúṅ (sngon byung) Cs. = sṅon-rábs (sngon rabs). -- sṅon-rábs (sngon rabs) ancient race, ancient history, antiquity, पुराण . -- sṅón-rol (sngon rol) (cf. sṅa-ról (snga rol)) former time or period, ma gtád-pai sṅón-rol źíg-tu (ma gtad pai sngon rol zhig tu) formerly, in former times, when (the chair) was not yet transferred (to...) Tar. -- dus na-níṅ sṅón-bai sṅón-rol-na (dus na ning sngon bai sngon rol na) a year ago (an expression with an unnecessary redundancy of words!) Mil. -- sṅon-lás (sngon las) former actions.
n. of a medicinal plant, acc. to Cs. poisonous; in Lh. Delphinium Cashmirianum, officinal. -- sṅón-bum (sngon bum) n. of a botanical work: 'the hundred thousand vegetables' Cs.
